title: T-130 â€” ESLint enum-lock guard for permissions.enum.ts (RBAC governance)
prompt:
# T-130 â€” ESLint enum-lock guard for `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts`

## Goal
Materialize the **ESLint enum-lock guard** that every per-module permission-enum task across the monorepo (01-npd T-101, 03-technical T-091, 04-planning-basic T-066, 05-warehouse T-058, 06-scanner-p1 T-049, 07-planning-ext T-058, 08-production T-056, 09-quality T-065, 14-multi-site T-031) currently cites as "02-settings T-046". T-046 is in fact `SET-006 Onboarding Completion`; the enum-lock guard does NOT exist yet. This task creates it.

The guard is a custom ESLint rule `no-direct-permissions-enum-edit` (delivered via the in-repo `tooling/eslint-rules/` workspace) that fails the lint job whenever `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts` is modified in a way that bypasses the canonical per-module workflow:
- Add-only edits must come through a PR labelled `permissions-enum-update` AND a corresponding `ALL_<MODULE>_PERMISSIONS` typed export.
- Renames, deletions, or weakening of the regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$` always fail (no escape hatch).
- The architect-only CODEOWNERS lock on the file (per T-001) remains the human gate; this rule is the **mechanised** gate that prevents merge-by-accident outside the approved label.

Per PRD `docs/prd/02-SETTINGS-PRD.md` Â§3 [D2] â€” "Permission model" / `permissions.enum.ts` is the single source-of-truth, flat dot-namespaced. The audit `_meta/audits/2026-05-14-permission-enum-addition.md` (RBAC permission-enum batch) declared this guard as a precondition; this task closes that gap.

## Files in scope
- `tooling/eslint-rules/package.json` [create â€” name `@monopilot/eslint-rules`, private, workspace package]
- `tooling/eslint-rules/src/no-direct-permissions-enum-edit.ts` [create â€” ESLint rule implementation]
- `tooling/eslint-rules/src/index.ts` [create â€” re-exports + plugin manifest]
- `tooling/eslint-rules/src/__tests__/no-direct-permissions-enum-edit.test.ts` [create â€” `RuleTester` (eslint) suite]
- `tooling/eslint-rules/tsconfig.json` [create]
- `packages/rbac/.eslintrc.cjs` [create or modify â€” register the plugin and enable the rule as `error`]
- `apps/web/.eslintrc.cjs` [modify â€” add `@monopilot/eslint-rules/no-direct-permissions-enum-edit` to the root config so `apps/web` import paths still trigger]
- `pnpm-workspace.yaml` [modify â€” add `tooling/eslint-rules` to the workspaces list if not already covered by a wildcard]
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` [modify â€” ensure `pnpm --filter @monopilot/eslint-rules test` runs in the lint job (or piggy-backs on the root `pnpm test` matrix)]

## Implementation contract
1. **Rule semantics** â€” `no-direct-permissions-enum-edit` runs on every file lint pass. It does NOT do anything when the file under lint is anything other than `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts`. When it IS that file:
   - Scan all top-level statements. If any export adds a string literal value whose dotted form fails the canonical regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$`, report `permissions-enum-regex-violation` with the offending value.
   - Detect rename/delete by comparing against a checked-in baseline at `tooling/eslint-rules/baselines/permissions.snapshot.json` â€” entries that disappear OR change their literal string fail with `permissions-enum-illegal-removal`.
   - If an `ALL_<MODULE>_PERMISSIONS` export is added, require: (a) it is a `readonly Permission[]` typed array literal, and (b) every element is also present in the parent `Permission` const. Failures report `permissions-enum-orphan-array`.
   - The PR-label gate is **CI-side**, NOT inside the rule (we can't read GitHub labels from ESLint). Instead the rule is `severity: error` always; the CI job under `.github/workflows/ci.yml` is the gate that decides whether to short-circuit by reading the `permissions-enum-update` label. Document this clearly in the rule docstring.
2. **Plugin packaging** â€” `tooling/eslint-rules` exports `module.exports = { rules: { 'no-direct-permissions-enum-edit': ruleImpl } }`. Add `"@monopilot/eslint-rules": "workspace:*"` to `packages/rbac/package.json` devDependencies.
3. **Baseline snapshot** â€” On first run (or via `pnpm --filter @monopilot/eslint-rules snapshot` script), regenerate `permissions.snapshot.json` from the current `permissions.enum.ts`. Snapshot is checked in; updates must accompany approved enum-update PRs.
4. **Tests** â€” `RuleTester` from `eslint`. Cases:
   - VALID: a snapshotted file unchanged.
   - VALID: a snapshotted file with an appended `npd.gate.approve` (matches regex AND present in `Permission`) AND a matching `ALL_NPD_PERMISSIONS` array.
   - INVALID: snapshot has `npd.gate.approve`, file removes it â†’ `permissions-enum-illegal-removal`.
   - INVALID: file adds `npd.gate` (2 segments) â†’ `permissions-enum-regex-violation`.
   - INVALID: file adds `npd.gate.approve` but no entry exists in `Permission` const â†’ `permissions-enum-orphan-array` (when consumed via the `ALL_<MODULE>_PERMISSIONS` array literal).
   - VALID: any OTHER file (e.g. `packages/rbac/src/role-mappings.ts`) â†’ rule no-ops.

## Acceptance criteria (Given-When-Then)
<!-- F15-consolidation 2026-05-14: 7 ACs fused to 4 via strict-subset fusion (F6/F8 patterns). AC5+AC1â†’AC1, AC2+AC3â†’AC2, AC4â†’AC3, AC7+AC6â†’AC4. All assertions remain testable. -->
- AC1 â€” Given the rule is loaded against any file other than `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts`, when lint runs, then the rule no-ops with zero reports; AND given `permissions.enum.ts` is unmodified and matches the checked-in snapshot, then `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rbac lint` also reports zero violations.
- AC2 â€” Given a developer renames or deletes an existing key in `permissions.enum.ts` without updating the snapshot, when lint runs, then the rule emits `permissions-enum-illegal-removal` with severity `error` and exits non-zero; AND given a developer appends a string that fails the regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$` (e.g. two-segment `npd.new_capability`), then the rule emits `permissions-enum-regex-violation` with severity `error`.
- AC3 â€” Given a developer adds a string to `ALL_<MODULE>_PERMISSIONS` but does NOT add it to the parent `Permission` const, when lint runs, then the rule emits `permissions-enum-orphan-array` with severity `error`.
- AC4 â€” Given `pnpm --filter @monopilot/eslint-rules vitest run` executes, then all `RuleTester` cases listed in `Implementation contract` Â§4 pass; AND given the PR carries the `permissions-enum-update` label AND the snapshot is regenerated via `pnpm --filter @monopilot/eslint-rules snapshot`, when CI re-runs, then both rule and CI gate accept the change.

## Test strategy (RED-first, runnable commands)
1. RED: write `tooling/eslint-rules/src/__tests__/no-direct-permissions-enum-edit.test.ts` covering all AC cases via ESLint's `RuleTester` + parallel vitest harness. Run:
   `pnpm --filter @monopilot/eslint-rules vitest run`
   â€” expect failure.
2. GREEN: implement the rule, plugin manifest, baseline snapshot, and registration in `packages/rbac/.eslintrc.cjs`. Re-run the same command â€” expect pass.
3. Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rbac lint` against an unmodified `permissions.enum.ts` â€” expect zero violations (AC1).
4. Run the integration assertion: temporarily delete one entry from `permissions.enum.ts`, re-run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rbac lint`, observe the `permissions-enum-illegal-removal` error, then revert.
5. Run `pnpm typecheck` for `@monopilot/eslint-rules` and `@monopilot/rbac` packages.

## Risk red lines
- Do not weaken the canonical regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$` â€” it must remain the literal source of truth in the rule body.
- Do not allow the rule to silently no-op on `permissions.enum.ts` due to a missing snapshot file; if the snapshot is missing, FAIL with a clear actionable message (`run: pnpm --filter @monopilot/eslint-rules snapshot`).
- Do not add a CLI escape hatch (`// eslint-disable-next-line`) that disables this rule â€” the rule MUST be marked `meta.docs.recommended: true` and the package README must instruct reviewers to reject PRs that include the disable comment for this rule.
- Do not depend on GitHub API calls or labels at lint time â€” label enforcement is a CI-job concern.
- Do not introduce a parallel permission registry file (no `packages/rbac/src/*PermissionEnum.ts`); the enum file remains the single source of truth.
- Do not commit a generated snapshot that omits any existing keys â€” snapshot updates must be additive and reviewer-verified.

## Closeout evidence required
- changed_files (with line counts)
- exact test commands and outputs (REDâ†’GREEN, vitest pass, `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rbac lint`)
- acceptance_criteria_status (one row per AC1..AC7)
- deviations_from_prd (or "none")
- git_status
- One end-to-end demo showing AC2 by intentionally renaming an entry and observing CI fail (gif or terminal capture stored under `_meta/handoffs/T-130-closeout/`).

Stack: TS + ESLint (>=9 flat-config compatible) + vitest + pnpm workspaces. Plugin built as a workspace package alongside `packages/rbac`.

pipeline_inputs.description: Create the ESLint enum-lock guard rule `no-direct-permissions-enum-edit` (in new `tooling/eslint-rules/` workspace) + baseline snapshot + register in packages/rbac/.eslintrc.cjs. Closes the missing precondition cited by every per-module permission-enum task across 8 modules + 14-multi-site T-031 (previously mis-anchored to 02-settings T-046, which is actually SET-006 Onboarding Completion).
pipeline_inputs.details: Materializes the ESLint enum-lock guard the audit `_meta/audits/2026-05-14-permission-enum-addition.md` and `_meta/audits/2026-05-14-review-R2-settings-finance-multisite.md` Â§3.1 declared missing. Rule lives at `tooling/eslint-rules/src/no-direct-permissions-enum-edit.ts`, runs on every lint pass, no-ops for any file other than `packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts`. It enforces: regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$` on additions; rename/delete detection against `tooling/eslint-rules/baselines/permissions.snapshot.json`; ALL_<MODULE>_PERMISSIONS typed-array consistency with parent `Permission` const. PR-label gating is delegated to CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml) â€” the rule itself is severity:error always. Companion `snapshot` script regenerates the baseline; snapshot updates accompany approved enum-update PRs. RuleTester suite covers all 6 invariant cases. Wires `@monopilot/eslint-rules` as a workspace package consumed by `packages/rbac/.eslintrc.cjs`. Foundation: this is the canonical mechanised gate that all 9 perm-enum tasks (01-npd T-101, 03-technical T-091, 04-planning-basic T-066, 05-warehouse T-058, 06-scanner-p1 T-049, 07-planning-ext T-058, 08-production T-056, 09-quality T-065 and 14-multi-site T-031) declare via cross_module_dependencies.
out_of_scope: Do not modify any existing permission string in packages/rbac/src/permissions.enum.ts (governance task, not content task).
Do not add a new permission namespace here â€” that is owned by the per-module perm-enum tasks (01-npd T-101 et al.).
Do not implement a runtime permission check helper (already owned by @monopilot/rbac core).
Do not introduce a 4-level matrix permission model (rejected per PRD Â§3 [D2]).
Do not extend the rule to cover unrelated files (single-purpose: permissions.enum.ts only).

cross_module_dependencies.reason: Consumes the guard via ALL_NPD_PERMISSIONS typed export validation.
Consumes the guard via ALL_TECHNICAL_PERMISSIONS typed export validation.
Consumes the guard via ALL_PLANNING_PERMISSIONS typed export validation.
Consumes the guard via ALL_WAREHOUSE_PERMISSIONS typed export validation.
Consumes the guard via ALL_SCANNER_PERMISSIONS typed export validation.
Consumes the guard via ALL_SCHEDULER_PERMISSIONS typed export validation.
Consumes the guard via ALL_PRODUCTION_PERMISSIONS typed export validation.

Consumes the guard via ALL_QUALITY_PERMISSIONS typed export validation. (Originally mis-anchored to T-037 by Pt4 audit â€” T-037 is the ncr_reports schema task. F10 reconciliation 2026-05-14 created the correct perm-enum task at T-065.)
Consumes the guard via ALL_MULTI_SITE_PERMISSIONS typed export validation.

acceptance_criteria: Given the rule is loaded against any non-target file it no-ops with zero reports AND given permissions.enum.ts is unmodified + matches snapshot then `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rbac lint` reports zero violations.

Given a rename/delete of an existing key without snapshot update lint emits `permissions-enum-illegal-removal` (severity error, exits non-zero) AND given a 2-segment addition failing `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$` lint emits `permissions-enum-regex-violation` (severity error).
Given an entry added to ALL_<MODULE>_PERMISSIONS but absent from the parent `Permission` const, when lint runs, then the rule emits `permissions-enum-orphan-array` with severity error.

Given `pnpm --filter @monopilot/eslint-rules vitest run` all RuleTester cases pass AND given the PR has the `permissions-enum-update` label + snapshot regenerated via the snapshot script CI accepts the change.
test_strategy: RED: write tooling/eslint-rules/src/__tests__/no-direct-permissions-enum-edit.test.ts covering all AC invariant cases (no-op on non-target, clean-pass on unmodified, illegal-removal, regex-violation, orphan-array, snapshot-update approval) via ESLint RuleTester. Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/eslint-rules vitest run`; expect failure.

GREEN: implement the rule, baseline snapshot, plugin manifest, and registration in packages/rbac/.eslintrc.cjs. Re-run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/eslint-rules vitest run`; expect pass. Vitest pass satisfies AC4 (RuleTester cases).
Integration: run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/rbac lint` against an unmodified `permissions.enum.ts` â€” expect zero violations (AC1).
Run `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` across the two packages; both must pass.
risk_red_lines: Do not weaken the regex `^[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+\.[a-z_]+$` in the rule body â€” it is the canonical SoT.
Do not allow the rule to silently no-op when the snapshot file is missing â€” must FAIL with an actionable error message instructing the reviewer to run the `snapshot` script.
Do not depend on GitHub API at lint time â€” PR-label enforcement is delegated to the CI job.
Do not introduce a parallel permission-registry file or a `// eslint-disable-next-line` escape hatch for this rule.
Do not commit a snapshot that omits existing keys â€” snapshot updates must be additive and reviewer-approved.
